0.body: The library allows to create scheduled tasks via Redis for Rust.
0.title: Introduction

1.body: Taskline revolves around the concept of a task. A task is a unit of work that is requested by a producer to be completed by a consumer / worker. A producer can schedule a task to be completed at a specific time in the future. A consumer can then fetch the task and complete it. There are backends for consumers and producers, which must impliment the DequeuBackend and EnqueuBackend traits. Right now, there is only one backend, which is Redis.
1.title: How does it work?

2.body: Taskline is a good fit for applications that need to schedule work to be done in the future. For example, Taskline is a good fit for: Scheduling emails to be sent in the future Scheduling a notification to be sent to a user in the future
2.breadcrumbs: Introduction » When should I use Taskline?
2.title: When should I use Taskline?

3.body: Actualy, Taskline uses a format of a backend. You can use any format which you want. There are two formats of a task for sending and receiving via Redis which are implemented in the library: JSON String
3.breadcrumbs: Introduction » A format of a task for sending and receiving via Redis
3.title: A format of a task for sending and receiving via Redis

4.body: If you want to delete a task from storage after handling, you can use RedisJsonBackend or RedisBackend with autodelete=false parameter. It's safe to use it only with one consumer. If you have more than one consumer, you can use distributed lock by redis. It's also named as redlock . See Distributed Locks with Redis . Don't forget to delete a task explicitly from storage after handling. See RedisBackend::delete. It's experimental implementation. In the future, it will be implemented more comfortable way.
4.breadcrumbs: Introduction » Deleting from storage after handling
4.title: Deleting from storage after handling

5.body: Taskline is provided as the a library on crates.io . To get started, add taskline as a dependency to your project. cargo add taskline
